validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_self_test_fixture -v passes 24/24 (0.005s, up from 11); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 344/345 with 1 OS-specific skip (38.5s, up from 332 -- 13 new cases for unit_055)

checkpoint.summary: unit_055 complete. Captured the /create-development-harness output artifacts for the self-test fixture: config.json (parallelism.enabled=true + full sub-field shape + deployment.target 'none' + pytest testing + 'claude-code' tool) and phase-graph.json (two phases PHASE_A 'items' + PHASE_B 'users', 6 units total, all parallel_safe=true, touches_paths populated to match README.md's ASCII graph exactly). unit_a3's description carries a 'SEEDED OVERLAP' callout + the exact 'path_overlap_with:unit_a2' exclusion string downstream operators will see. unit_b2's description carries a 'SEEDED SCOPE VIOLATION' callout + instructs the sub-agent to also write src/seeds/users.json + names the expected 'scope_violation' conflict category. Rather than running /create-development-harness interactively (which would require simulating a multi-step question session), unit_055 captures the OUTPUTS /create would produce against this fixture with the documented answer set -- reproducible, diff-able, grep-able. test_self_test_fixture.py grew from 11 to 24 cases: new TestFixtureConfigJson class (4 cases pinning schema v2, parallelism enabled, sub-field shape, not-deploy-affecting), new TestFixturePhaseGraphJson class (9 cases pinning schema v2, phase structure, 6-unit set, every unit parallel_safe=true, seeded overlap, seeded scope violation via fnmatch-based-pattern-check matching runtime semantics, both SEEDED callouts in descriptions). Suite 332 -> 345.
checkpoint.next_action: Complete unit_056 (PHASE_013): run /invoke-development-harness iteratively against the fixture until all 2 phases complete. The run must actually exercise: (1) at least one batch of size >= 2 (expected: unit_a1 + unit_a2 in PHASE_A's first batch); (2) the seeded overlap-matrix rejection (unit_a3 excluded from that batch with path_overlap_with:unit_a2, then picked up in the next turn); (3) the seeded scope violation (unit_b2 dispatched, sub-agent writes src/seeds/users.json beyond declared scope, merge_batch._scope_violations rejects with scope_violation category). Captures trace log showing each batch composition + merge outcome under fixtures/self-test/trace.log. Validation: trace log contents contain each seeded condition fired + final fleet.mode 'idle' + no orphan worktrees/branches after the run. This unit is an OPERATOR TASK -- simulating a full invoke session against the fixture. In the dogfood loop context, I should write a Python test harness that exercises the three seeded conditions programmatically (via compute_parallel_batch + dispatch_batch + merge_batch calls in a temp repo) instead of requiring human /invoke iteration.
